title: Aureline signed policy-bundle boundary schema
description: Boundary schema for one signed policy_bundle_record frozen in docs/identity/offline_entitlement_and_policy_seed.md. A policy bundle is the versioned, signed, inspectable object a self-hosted or managed deployment distributes to Aureline clients so that policy decisions, managed-capability narrowing, and workspace-trust restrictions ride on one auditable artifact instead of surface-local toggles. One record carries policy-source provenance, signer identity, signer continuity state, scope selector (tenant or org, deployment-profile scope, install-profile linkage), bundle version, policy epoch, last-known-good linkage, distribution posture across live / mirror / file-import / offline paths, effective and expiry windows, revocation refs, downgrade-safety posture, local-only-lane posture, and explicit local-versus-vendor-console explainability so an enterprise claim about narrowing or widening can be justified without privileged console-only state. Raw bundle bytes, raw signatures, raw policy rule bodies, raw OIDC tokens, raw claim payloads, raw directory attributes, and raw tenant directory material never cross this boundary; the record carries opaque refs, typed vocabulary, and reviewable-sentence summaries only. Live OIDC, SCIM, and provider implementations are out of scope at this revision. Adding a new policy_source_class, signer_authority_class, signer_continuity_state, distribution_source_class, distribution_freshness_class, bundle_validation_state, scope_selector_class, policy_capability_class, revocation_reason_class, downgrade_safety_posture, or local_vs_vendor_console_explainability value is additive-minor and requires a policy_bundle_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row co-signed by security_trust_review and product_scope_review.

policy_bundle_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this policy-bundle export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in admin exports, support bundles, release evidence, and boundary-manifest rows. Raw bundle bytes, raw signatures, raw policy rule bodies, and raw directory attributes never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short, reviewable sentence safe for banners, admin exports, support packets, and release evidence. Raw policy rule text, raw claim payloads, and raw secret material MUST NOT appear.

export_safe_label.description: Short, export-safe label for banners and admin consoles. Raw URLs, raw tenant ids, raw directory attribute values, and raw signing material MUST NOT appear.

identity_mode.description: Identity mode re-exported from ADR-0001. account_free_local never has a policy bundle bound to it except as an inspectable informational artifact; self_hosted_org uses a customer-run source; managed_workspace is the vendor-hosted convenience layer layered over self_hosted_org.

deployment_profile_class.description: Frozen deployment-profile vocabulary reused across policy bundle, entitlement snapshot, admin audit packet, and boundary manifest surfaces.

workspace_trust_state.description: Workspace trust state re-exported from ADR-0001. A policy bundle MAY narrow trusted-workspace behaviour but MUST NOT widen it and MUST NOT remove the restricted-mode read / search / edit / save guarantees.

redaction_class.description: Redaction class re-exported from the lifecycle/support/release vocabulary.

policy_context.description: Policy, identity-mode, and trust-state context the record was minted under.

policy_epoch_ref.description: Stable epoch identifier of the form policy_epoch.<lane>.<windowed>.<id>. An epoch identifies a contiguous active window for the bundle; a new epoch is minted on every rotation, rotation-class signer change, or substantive policy change. Consumers never concatenate or parse the epoch beyond equality and 'newer than' ordering carried elsewhere in the record.

bundle_version_ref.description: Bundle version identifier of the form <bundle_name>@<semver>. The semver segment follows <major>.<minor>.<patch> with an optional pre-release suffix. Diffs are mechanical across versions; support / admin-export rows cite the exact version.

policy_source_class.description: Closed policy-source vocabulary. customer_self_hosted_origin is the ADR-0001 self_hosted_org default. vendor_managed_origin is the managed_convenience layer. signed_mirror_origin is a customer- or vendor-operated mirror of an authoritative source. manual_file_import_origin is a file-import posture for bounded-network deployments. air_gapped_transfer_origin names an offline signed transfer. runtime_preload_origin names a preload bundle shipped with the build and used only as a last-known-good seed before the first live fetch.

signer_authority_class.description: Closed signer-authority vocabulary describing who minted the bundle. customer_operated_trust_root is the self-hosted case. vendor_operated_trust_root is the managed case. cross_signed_transition_root is admissible only during a signer rotation window. managed_admin_delegated_signer is a named admin delegation scoped by signing_scope_note. build_preload_signer is the last-known-good seed class shipped with the build.

signer_continuity_state_class.description: Closed signer-continuity vocabulary. Distinct from freshness: a mirrored snapshot may carry continuity proof while still being past its freshness grace.

signer_authority_ref.description: Opaque ref to the signer or trust root the record expects to be current. Null is admissible only for air_gapped_transfer_origin or runtime_preload_origin variants where signer identity resolves by separate continuity statement.

previous_signer_refs.description: Prior signer refs relevant to the continuity story.

continuity_statement_ref.description: Opaque ref to the continuity or rotation statement. Null when continuity_state does not rely on a statement.

last_verified_at.description: When signer continuity was last verified on the producing path. Null is admissible for imported / offline states where only historic continuity evidence exists.

signer_identity.description: Signer identity and continuity envelope. Raw signing material never appears here.

scope_selector_class.description: Closed scope-selector vocabulary. local_advisory_scope_only is the posture that applies to inspectable-only bundles used by account_free_local lanes for visibility without any authority over managed-gated features.

tenant_or_org_ref.description: Opaque ref to the tenant or org the bundle is scoped to. Null for local_advisory_scope_only and for deployment-profile-only scopes.

install_profile_card_refs.description: Opaque refs into artifacts/release/install_topology_matrix.yaml. Empty is admissible when the bundle applies across all install-profile cards in scope.

workspace_refs.description: Opaque refs to specific workspace ids the bundle narrows to. Empty is admissible when the bundle applies across every workspace in scope.

inherits_parent_scope_ref.description: Opaque ref to a parent scope this bundle inherits from. Non-null only when selector_class is tenant_or_org_scoped or deployment_profile_scoped.

scope_selector.description: Scope selector envelope. Raw tenant names, raw directory attribute values, and raw workspace paths never appear here; every binding is an opaque ref.

policy_capability_class.description: Closed policy-capability vocabulary listing the capability classes a bundle may narrow or widen. widening narrowing rules are expressed per-capability inside narrowing_rule.

narrowing_direction_class.description: Direction a capability rule moves the effective posture. narrows_only is the default admissible direction; widens_only_within_self_hosted_ceiling is admissible only when the widening stays at or below the self_hosted_org ceiling frozen in ADR-0001. informational_only is the inspectable advisory posture.

reversible_on_downgrade.description: True when downgrading from managed_workspace to self_hosted_org or to account_free_local reverses the narrowing. False names a narrowing that persists until superseded.

distribution_source_class.description: How the local client / admin saw this policy bundle.

distribution_path_class.description: Closed distribution-path vocabulary, parallel to schemas/security/emergency_action_record.

distribution_freshness_class.description: Closed freshness vocabulary. An imported snapshot MUST NOT present as authoritative_live.

bundle_validation_state_class.description: Closed validation-state vocabulary for one distribution path.

receipt_ref.description: Stable ref preserved by mirror-import rows, manual-import receipts, admin exports, and support packets that quote this distribution state.

distribution_status_record.description: One live / mirror / manual-import / offline projection row for the same policy bundle.

last_known_good_bundle_ref.description: Opaque ref to the last bundle this record supersedes under same-signer-chain continuity. Null only for the first bundle ever observed under a new signer.

fallback_posture_note.description: How the client falls back to last-known-good when refresh fails: which narrowings persist, which widen, and when visible recovery is required.

last_known_good_linkage.description: Last-known-good linkage. The bundle a client falls back to when a refresh fails. ADR-0001 pins that expired bundles degrade to the safe defaults documented by the bundle itself rather than a hard lockout, unless the bundle was explicitly configured to fail closed.

revocation_reason_class.description: Closed revocation-reason vocabulary for referencing emergency-action / revocation records against a bundle.

revocation_ref.description: Opaque ref into schemas/security/emergency_action_record.schema.json#revocation_record that revokes this bundle.

downgrade_safety_posture_class.description: Closed downgrade-safety vocabulary. fail_safe_local_continuity guarantees ADR-0001 local-core capabilities on expiry. configured_fail_closed_restricted is admissible only when the bundle explicitly opts into fail-closed behaviour. posture_unresolved_review_required fails closed with a visible repair hook; it is never a silent default.

local_only_lane_posture_class.description: Posture reviewers audit to spot bundles whose defaults would be coercive or ambiguous on an account_free_local lane. Anything other than local_only_safe_default or local_only_inspectable_only requires a justification note.

local_vs_vendor_console_explainability_c.description: Closed local-versus-vendor-console vocabulary. locally_explainable_from_bundle is the default and the value required for every enterprise narrowing claim; vendor_console_only_acknowledged_gap is admissible only when a gap is explicitly acknowledged and a separately-reviewed waiver names the closing plan.

locally_inspectable_fields.description: Fields a local inspector, CLI, support-export surface, or admin desktop can render from this bundle without calling the vendor console.

vendor_console_only_fields.description: Fields only the vendor console can render at full detail. An empty list is required when explainability_class is locally_explainable_from_bundle.

acknowledged_gap_waiver_refs.description: Opaque refs into artifacts/governance/exception_packet rows acknowledging an explainability gap. Required to be non-empty when explainability_class is vendor_console_only_acknowledged_gap.

review_deadline_at.description: When the next mandatory review of this bundle must happen. Null when the bundle does not carry a review obligation (runtime_preload_origin seeds).

expires_at.description: When the bundle expires if not superseded. Null when the bundle is an open-ended seed.

grace_window_note.description: Plain-language description of what the grace window guarantees while the bundle is between expires_at and a successor bundle.

fixture_metadata.description: Optional worked-example prelude carried on fixtures.

policy_bundle_id.description: Stable id for this policy bundle, preserved across bundle_version rolls within the same signer chain. Mint a new id only under cross-signed or broken continuity.

policy_source_label.description: Export-safe label naming the authoritative source of the bundle (for example 'Customer OIDC policy origin'). Raw URLs, raw hostnames, and raw tenant names MUST NOT appear.

narrowing_rules.description: Capability-level narrowing rules. Every rule states which capability class it addresses, which direction it moves the posture, whether a downgrade reverses it, and a reviewable note.

revocation_refs.description: Zero or more revocation refs into emergency_action_record.revocation_record. A bundle is considered revoked when any entry is present; consumers render the most recent by revoked_at.

local_only_lane_note.description: Justification that makes the local-only-lane posture reviewable. Anything other than local_only_safe_default or local_only_inspectable_only MUST explain why the posture is honest rather than coercive or ambiguous.

allOf.description: account_free_local identity mode MUST use local_advisory_scope_only scope and MUST remain inspectable-only on the local-only lane.

A stale_past_grace or offline_snapshot_expired distribution status MUST NOT claim authoritative-live freshness and MUST NOT be the only distribution row if the bundle is being relied on for managed narrowing.
A bundle that carries any revocation_refs MUST declare a posture that does not introduce new managed narrowing for clients holding stale copies.
explainability_class must be locally_explainable_from_bundle, locally_explainable_with_signed_evidence, or acknowledged-gap with waiver refs.

policy_bundle_record.description: Policy-bundle record. One record per versioned signed policy bundle. Carries the policy source, signer identity and continuity state, scope selector, bundle version, policy epoch, per-capability narrowing rules, distribution status across live / mirror / file-import / offline paths, last-known-good linkage, review schedule, revocation refs, downgrade-safety posture, local-only-lane posture, and the local-versus-vendor-console explainability envelope so an enterprise narrowing or widening claim can be justified from the bundle alone.
